title: Look inside a new waterfront hotel on Lake Champlain
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Harborvale, a 161-room hotel, opened in Burlington, Vermont, offering lakeside views and proximity to Church Street Marketplace.
Developed by Westport Hospitality, The Harborvale features event spaces, a restaurant, pool, and is part of Marriott's Autograph Collection.
Rates range from $299-$499+ per night, with amenities including a fitness center, dog-friendly policies, and exclusive local collaboration packages.

title: Woman charged after deadly pedestrian crash on Nantucket
author: John Waller
bullets: Kathleen M. Featherston, 62, from Fairfield, Connecticut, charged with negligent operation in a deadly Nantucket crash.
The crash on June 29 in a Stop & Shop parking lot resulted in the death of Stacey J. Rosen.
Featherston's charges were delayed due to issues in obtaining evidence from her vehicle's manufacturer.

title: Trump denies Mass. disaster relief for Feb. blizzard; Healey says she will appeal
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: President Trump denied Massachusetts disaster aid for snow removal costs from February's blizzard, prompting an appeal from Gov. Maura Healey.
Trump's denial of aid included New York, New Jersey, and Rhode Island, while approving requests from nine Republican-leaning states.
A POLITICO analysis showed Trump approving 23% of aid requests from Democrat-governed states versus 89% from Republican-led states.

title: Local obituary: Donato Ventura, aka Danny V., longtime Boston Herald high school sports reporter
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Donato 'Danny V.' Ventura, Boston Herald high school sports reporter, died at 66 after battling cancer.
Ventura was born in Brighton, Massachusetts, and worked at the Boston Herald for over 35 years.
Funeral services will be held in Brighton with interment in Newton; donations can be made to the American Cancer Society.

title: Boxford woman linked to two other embezzlement cases charged with stealing thousands from employer
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Stephanie Hirshon, 53, of Boxford, charged with larceny over $1,200, implicated in three embezzlement cases.
Hirshon allegedly stole $120,000 from employer and $50,000 from Framingham Chamber; also investigated in Boxford School fraud.
Hirshon confessed to unauthorized payments of $17,500, claiming it was to pay for college and escape abuse.

title: The best movies on Netflix right now (July 2026)
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix's library includes over 3,500 films, categorized by genre to assist viewers in finding top content easily.
Featured movies include 'Gone Girl' (2014), starring Ben Affleck and Rosamund Pike, exploring themes of media and marriage.
'A League of Their Own' (1992), starring Tom Hanks and Geena Davis, depicts a women's pro baseball team during WWII.

title: The best pet-friendly hotel in America is in Boston, according to USA Today readers
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Fairmont Copley Plaza in Boston named best pet-friendly hotel in America by USA Today readers.
Inn by the Sea in Maine and Paws on Pelham in Newport also featured in the top 10 list.
Cori Copley, the Canine Ambassador at Fairmont Copley Plaza, greets guests as part of the hotel's pet-friendly offerings.

title: ‘There’s no loyalty’: Jaylen Brown casually discussed trade while at the World Cup
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Jaylen Brown discussed his recent trade from the Celtics to the 76ers while at the World Cup in Foxborough.
Brown expressed surprise at the trade, commenting, 'There's no loyalty, there's no love' in basketball.
Brown clarified to YouTuber IShowSpeed that Paul George had been traded to Boston in the same deal.

title: Here were some of the celebs on hand to watch France and Morocco’s World Cup quarterfinal at ‘Boston Stadium’
author: John Waller
bullets: France defeated Morocco 2-0 in a World Cup quarterfinal at 'Boston Stadium' in Foxborough.
Celebrities attending included Vermont singer Noah Kahan, artist Shakira, and former Liverpool manager Jürgen Klopp.
France's Antoine Griezmann and Kylian Mbappé's parents were present to support the national team.

title: Boston Councilor Julia Mejia in ‘high spirits’ after medical emergency caused by dehydration and exhaustion
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Councilor Julia Mejia collapsed in City Hall due to dehydration and exhaustion during a council meeting.
Mejia is in stable condition and recovering, expressing gratitude for support and intending a return to work soon.
First elected in 2019, Mejia is known for her progressive policies and advocacy within the Boston City Council.
